clinical_history: The following ECG shows which diagnosis?
diagnosis: c) The ECG shows prominent Q waves with ST elevations and/or T wave inversions in leads V1-V4 and aVL.  There is also sinus bradycardia, slight PR prolongation and probable left atrial abnormality.  Pathologic Q waves in V1-V3 are classically attributed to "anteroseptal" myocardial infarction (MI).  However, angiographic and echocardiographic evidence indicates that Q waves in V1-V3/V4 more often correlate with infarction involving the apical and sometimes even the lateral wall of the left ventricle. (Bogaty P, et al.  Is anteroseptal myocardial infarction an appropriate term?  Am J Med 2002;113:37-41.)
